message:
test(fnmatch): epic completion — GNU + property regression suite (bd-fnm-3)

Final sub-bead of bd-fnm-epic. Adds 4 conformance regression tests
to lock in the core-port behavior:

- diff_fnmatch_leading_dir: FNM_LEADING_DIR across 7 cases incl
  "a/b" vs "a/b/c/d" — verifies pattern matches dir prefixes per
  glibc semantics
- diff_fnmatch_file_name_alias: confirms FNM_FILE_NAME (1<<0) is a
  glibc alias for FNM_PATHNAME with identical match behavior
- diff_fnmatch_property_pattern_against_self: literal-escape every
  metachar in 6 strings, verify pattern matches itself — property
  test for escape correctness
- diff_fnmatch_star_chain_against_arbitrary_text: 7 "*x*y*z*"
  patterns against synthetic corpus

All 7 conformance tests pass + 16 core unit tests = 23 tests for
the fnmatch family, 0 divergences against glibc.

Closes bd-fnm-3 and bd-fnm-epic. Family now 100% native Rust:
- core/string/fnmatch.rs   FnmatchFlags + engine, 471 lines + 16 tests
- abi/string_abi.rs::fnmatch  reduced from ~280 lines to 14-line shim

Improvements over previous abi-only impl:
- Independently unit-testable (16 tests in core, ran without FFI)
- Pure-safe Rust (slice indexing, no pointer arithmetic)
- LEADING_DIR semantics fully exercised (was previously implicit)
- FnmatchFlags as a typed wrapper instead of c_int bag
